project.name: PoPunkouterSoftware
project.description: A .NET 10 Blazor Web App with an ASP.NET Core server host, a WebAssembly client, and operational endpoints for Azure reporting, diagnostics, GitHub activity, and infrastructure scanning. Runs locally on HTTP port 8000 with health and diagnostics endpoints.

nodes.name: _helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Playwright test helper that wires up page error and console listeners so specs can assert the absence of runtime JS errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Subscribes to Playwright Page 'pageerror' and 'console' events to accumulate runtime errors.

nodes.name: collectJsErrors
nodes.summary: Attaches pageerror and console listeners to a Playwright Page and returns a mutable array that collects error messages for later assertions.

nodes.name: auth-model-lobby.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end spec covering authentication, model selection, and lobby flows, using the shared error-collection helper to guard against runtime JS errors.

nodes.name: pages.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright end-to-end spec verifying that the Home, Azure Dashboard, and Diag pages load without JS errors, return HTTP 200, hydrate the Blazor WASM client, and render correctly on mobile viewports.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Playwright test.describe grouping, role-based locators, and viewport overrides to test Blazor WebAssembly hydration.

nodes.name: smoke-regressions.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright smoke and regression spec that exercises core application routes to catch broken pages and previously fixed defects, relying on the shared JS-error helper.

nodes.name: deploy.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and deploys the .NET 10 Blazor app to Azure App Service on push to master, using OIDC authentication and a protected 'production' environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OIDC federated credentials (id-token: write) instead of stored Azure secrets, pins all third-party actions to commit SHAs, and gates deploys behind a GitHub production environment with concurrency cancellation.

nodes.name: build-and-deploy
nodes.summary: Single CI/CD job that validates deploy targets, restores, builds, and publishes the solution, then authenticates to Azure via OIDC and deploys the published artifacts to Azure App Service.

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Describes the four runtime layers of the solution (host, WASM client, shared contracts, infrastructure) and the Azure reporting flow from AzureReportService through AzureReportStore and diagnostics endpoints to the hidden /azure dashboard.

nodes.name: CONVENTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Coding conventions for the project covering feature/infrastructure layout, immutable shared models, async and cancellation patterns, input validation, opt-in destructive Azure operations, and UI styling tokens.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.sln
nodes.summary: Visual Studio solution file aggregating the host, client, shared, infrastructure, and three test projects (unit, integration, E2E) with their build configurations.
nodes.languageNotes: MSBuild solution file enumerating projects and per-configuration build mappings.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project overview documenting the solution layout, local development prerequisites and commands, runtime notes (health/diagnostics endpoints, Azure behavior), testing strategy, and developer guidance.

nodes.name: STACK.md
nodes.summary: Enumerates the technology stack: .NET 10 / C# 14, ASP.NET Core with Blazor WebAssembly, Radzen.Blazor, Azure SDKs, Serilog/OpenTelemetry, and the xUnit/Playwright test tooling.

nodes.name: TESTING.md
nodes.summary: Describes the testing strategy across unit, integration (WebApplicationFactory in the Testing environment), and Playwright E2E tests, plus required build verification and runtime checks for browser-facing changes.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose definition running an Azurite container that emulates Azure Blob, Queue, and Table Storage on ports 10000-10002 for local development and integration tests, backed by a named volume.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-service compose stack exposing Azurite storage emulator endpoints with a persistent named volume.

nodes.name: global.json
nodes.summary: Pins the .NET SDK to version 10.0.300 with latestMinor roll-forward, ensuring consistent build tooling across environments.

nodes.name: modern-ui.base.css
nodes.summary: Foundation stylesheet of the modern-ui design system defining CSS custom properties (design tokens for colors, spacing, radii, fonts, motion) and base element/layout styling, including Radzen token unification.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses :root CSS custom properties extensively to drive a cohesive dark theme and remaps Radzen component tokens (--rz-*) onto the app's own tokens.

nodes.name: modern-ui.components.css
nodes.summary: Component-level stylesheet for the modern-ui design system, styling cards, buttons, grids, and other UI widgets using the shared design tokens defined in the base stylesheet.

nodes.name: modern-ui.css
nodes.summary: Aggregator stylesheet that imports the base, components, and responsive partials to expose the complete modern-ui design system through a single entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure @import barrel that composes the design system from three partials, mirroring a JS barrel file pattern in CSS.

nodes.name: modern-ui.responsive.css
nodes.summary: Responsive layer of the modern-ui design system providing media-query overrides that adapt the layout and components across breakpoints.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.csproj
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core Web SDK project file for the host server; references the Shared, Client, and Infrastructure projects and pulls in Blazor WASM hosting, Radzen UI, Serilog, OpenTelemetry/Azure Monitor, OpenAPI/Scalar, and Azure Key Vault/Identity packages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses central package management (versions in Directory.Packages.props) and InternalsVisibleTo to expose internals to the test projects; copies an automation PowerShell script to output.

nodes.name: appsettings.json
nodes.summary: Base runtime configuration for the host server covering Serilog logging, allowed hosts/origins, Azure Table Storage, Application Insights, feature flags, Azure OpenAI, GitHub, the service pinger, incidents, and Key Vault settings.

nodes.name: appsettings.Development.json
nodes.summary: Development environment overrides for the host server, adjusting logging levels, Serilog, Key Vault, Azure Table Storage, feature flags, and Application Insights for local debugging.

nodes.name: New-AzureEfficiencyReport.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell automation that probes an Azure subscription via the Azure CLI for inventory, cost, efficiency, governance, and waste signals, rendering an interactive HTML report and a commented-out cleanup script.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Azure Resource Graph paging with skip-tokens, here-strings for HTML/JS templating, and emits an embedded Tailwind/JS report with client-side CSV export.

nodes.name: Invoke-AzCliJson
nodes.summary: Wrapper that invokes the Azure CLI with JSON output, handling non-zero exit codes by either warning (AllowFailure) or throwing, then deserializing the response.

nodes.name: Invoke-AzCliText
nodes.summary: Invokes the Azure CLI returning trimmed text output, with optional failure tolerance for non-critical commands.

nodes.name: Get-ObjectProperty
nodes.summary: Resolves the first non-empty value among candidate dotted property paths on a PowerShell object, used to normalize varying Azure CLI response shapes.

nodes.name: ConvertTo-SafeDouble
nodes.summary: Parses a value into a double using invariant culture, returning a default for null, empty, or sentinel strings like 'none' and 'n/a'.

nodes.name: Get-ExpirationStatus
nodes.summary: Classifies a Unix-epoch expiration timestamp as expired, expiring within 30 days, active, or having no expiration.

nodes.name: Get-MetricSummary
nodes.summary: Queries Azure Monitor metrics for a resource over the lookback window and aggregates each metric's average, total, and data point count.

nodes.name: New-AzureNamingStandardizationPlan.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell tool that parses the embedded inventory JSON from the efficiency report and produces a CAF-derived naming/region standardization plan as CSV, Markdown, and a non-destructive remediation script.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex extraction over generated HTML to recover the inventory JSON, then switch-based naming derivation following Cloud Adoption Framework abbreviations.

nodes.name: ConvertTo-Slug
nodes.summary: Normalizes a resource name into a lowercase hyphenated slug, stripping rg-/po- prefixes and collapsing non-alphanumeric runs.

nodes.name: Get-RegionCode
nodes.summary: Maps an Azure location name to its short region code (e.g., westus2 to wus2), falling back to a sanitized form for unknown regions.

nodes.name: Get-ResourceAbbreviation
nodes.summary: Returns the CAF-style resource abbreviation (st, app, asp, kv, etc.) for a given Azure resource type via regex switch.

nodes.name: Get-AppFromResource
nodes.summary: Infers the owning application identifier for a resource from its resource group and name, stripping common type prefixes.

nodes.name: Get-Role
nodes.summary: Derives a role token (api, web, linux, health, ops, etc.) for a resource based on its type and name patterns.

nodes.name: Get-TargetName
nodes.summary: Constructs the standardized target resource name from app, environment, region code, and resource abbreviation, enforcing Azure length limits per resource type.

nodes.name: Get-Action
nodes.summary: Determines the migration action text for a resource by comparing current name/group/region against the proposed targets and applying recreate-and-migrate guidance per resource type.

nodes.name: azure-naming-remediation.ps1
nodes.summary: Generated, intentionally non-destructive remediation script containing commented-out Azure CLI commands to create standardized resource groups and tag resources for the naming migration.
nodes.languageNotes: Output artifact produced by New-AzureNamingStandardizationPlan.ps1; every command is commented for safety and must be reviewed before execution.

nodes.name: azure-naming-standardization-plan.md
nodes.summary: Generated Markdown report listing the proposed CAF-derived naming/region baseline and a per-resource-group migration table mapping current resources to standardized targets and actions.

nodes.name: azure.yaml
nodes.summary: Azure Developer CLI (azd) project configuration declaring the project name, metadata, infra provider, and service definitions for deployment.

nodes.name: cleanup_suggestions.ps1
nodes.summary: Generated placeholder cleanup script containing only commented header lines; populated by New-AzureEfficiencyReport.ps1 with reviewable delete/stop commands for orphaned or idle Azure resources.

nodes.name: setup.ps1
nodes.summary: First-run developer setup script that frees dev ports, verifies winget/Docker availability, and ensures Azure CLI authentication for Key Vault access.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern list controlling which paths the Understand-Anything knowledge-graph tooling excludes from scanning.

nodes.name: Directory.Build.props
nodes.summary: Solution-wide MSBuild properties applied to every project, setting shared build settings such as target framework, nullable, and language version.
nodes.languageNotes: Directory.Build.props is auto-imported by MSBuild for all projects under its directory tree.

nodes.name: Directory.Packages.props
nodes.summary: Central Package Management manifest pinning NuGet package versions for all projects in the solution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .NET Central Package Management (CPM) so individual csproj files reference packages without versions.

nodes.name: config-inventory.md
nodes.summary: Design note proposing an Azure custom config audit page, covering problem statement, recommended direction, MVP scope, and open questions.

nodes.name: bind-managed-identity.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell infrastructure script that binds a managed identity to Azure resources for keyless authentication.

nodes.name: migrate-kv-prefix.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell infrastructure script that migrates Azure Key Vault secrets to a new naming prefix convention.

nodes.name: _Imports.razor
nodes.summary: Global Razor using-directives imported by every component in the Blazor WebAssembly client project.
nodes.languageNotes: Blazor _Imports.razor cascades @using directives to all sibling and descendant components.

nodes.name: MainLayout.razor
nodes.summary: Root application layout providing the navigation shell, header, and body render fragment for all pages.

nodes.name: MainLayout.razor.css
nodes.summary: Scoped CSS styling the MainLayout component's shell, header, and content regions.
nodes.languageNotes: Blazor scoped CSS is isolated to its paired component via generated attribute selectors.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.Charts.cs
nodes.summary: Partial class of the AzureDashboard page that builds chart datasets and badge/label helpers for resource visualizations.
nodes.languageNotes: C# partial class spreading the AzureDashboard component across multiple files by concern.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.Operations.cs
nodes.summary: Partial class of the AzureDashboard page computing operational health reports, cost-watch items, and maintenance-refresh signals.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.Portfolio.cs
nodes.summary: Partial class of the AzureDashboard page that derives the portfolio view: safe-to-remove candidates, consolidated services, priority queue, and resource inference heuristics.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.razor
nodes.summary: Markup for the Azure operations dashboard page rendering fleet health, charts, portfolio tables, and detail tabs.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.razor.cs
nodes.summary: Primary code-behind for the AzureDashboard page handling report loading, SignalR-driven refresh progress, navigation guards, and lifecycle.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements IAsyncDisposable and uses a SignalR HubConnection for live refresh progress.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard.Trends.cs
nodes.summary: Partial class of the AzureDashboard page loading historical scan summaries and building time-series trend data for cost, uptime, and response history.

nodes.name: Index.razor
nodes.summary: Landing/portfolio page that fetches the live Azure app inventory and renders it as a hero section plus a grid of portfolio app cards.

nodes.name: SafeToRemoveItem.cs
nodes.summary: Data model record describing an Azure resource flagged as safe to remove, with its type, name, and removal reasons.

nodes.name: PortfolioModels.cs
nodes.summary: Data models for the portfolio view, defining AppsWrapper and AppModel records used to deserialize and render the app inventory.

nodes.name: AppModel
nodes.summary: Record describing a single portfolio app entry with name, status, description, URL, technologies, and GitHub repo.

nodes.name: AzureDashboardDetailTabs.razor
nodes.summary: Tabbed detail section component for the Azure dashboard, rendering drill-down tables for services, costs, SSL, and trends.

nodes.name: PortfolioAppCard.razor
nodes.summary: Reusable card component that renders a single portfolio app's details, status badge, technologies, and links.

nodes.name: PortfolioAppCard.razor.css
nodes.summary: Scoped CSS styling the PortfolioAppCard component's layout and visual treatment.

nodes.name: DevErrorBoundary.razor
nodes.summary: Development-mode error boundary component that catches and displays component exceptions during local debugging.

nodes.name: PoAppSession.cs
nodes.summary: Client-side session service persisting user session state to browser localStorage via JS interop.

nodes.name: PoAppSession
nodes.summary: Service that gets, saves, and clears the user's session in browser localStorage using JSON serialization and JS interop.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.Client.csproj
nodes.summary: Project file for the Blazor WebAssembly client, declaring the WASM SDK and package references such as Radzen.

nodes.name: Program.cs
nodes.summary: Blazor WebAssembly entry point wiring up the HttpClient, Radzen UI services, and PoAppSession before running the host.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level statements with WebAssemblyHostBuilder configure DI for the WASM client.

nodes.name: AzureDashboard
nodes.summary: Code-behind component class for the Azure dashboard, orchestrating report loading, SignalR refresh progress, and navigation guards across several partial files.

nodes.name: LoadReportAsync
nodes.summary: Fetches the Azure report JSON from the server and derives consolidated services and safe-to-remove items for display.

nodes.name: RefreshAsync
nodes.summary: Triggers a server-side subscription scan, tracking live progress over SignalR with cancellation and timeout handling.

nodes.name: EnsureHubConnectedAsync
nodes.summary: Builds and starts the SignalR hub connection, wiring progress event handlers that update the dashboard's refresh state.

nodes.name: WaitForRefreshCompletionAsync
nodes.summary: Polls the server for refresh completion, honoring cancellation and a configurable delay between attempts.

nodes.name: DownloadReportAsync
nodes.summary: Serializes the current report to JSON and triggers a browser download via JS interop.

nodes.name: BuildSafeToRemove
nodes.summary: Scans inventory for orphaned plans, AI services, and log workspaces to build a deduplicated, prioritized safe-to-remove list.

nodes.name: BuildConsolidatedServices
nodes.summary: Groups raw web services by canonical app key and aggregates traffic, response time, and health into consolidated service rows.

nodes.name: BuildPriorityQueue
nodes.summary: Builds a ranked action queue from unhealthy services, expiring SSL certs, and orphaned resources, ordered by impact score.

nodes.name: BuildHealthReport
nodes.summary: Computes a fleet health report card with plan/AI/log counts and generates contextual recommendations.

nodes.name: LoadHistoryAsync
nodes.summary: Fetches historical scan summaries from the server to populate trend charts.

nodes.name: WasmRenderModes.cs
nodes.summary: Defines a small static helper exposing the InteractiveWebAssembly render mode used to configure Blazor component interactivity in the client.
nodes.languageNotes: Static class centralizing the InteractiveWebAssembly render mode constant.

nodes.name: apps.json
nodes.summary: Static portfolio data file listing the catalog of applications (name, status, description, URL, technologies, GitHub repo) rendered by the client portfolio UI.

nodes.name: azure-full-report.json
nodes.summary: Large pre-generated snapshot of the Azure inventory report (web services, costs, SSL expiry, config drift, storage, AI services, downtime diagnoses, plan recommendations) served statically as the dashboard fallback dataset.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors the AzureReport model shape produced by AzureReportService for offline/static rendering.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Root HTML host page for the Blazor WebAssembly client, loading the app stylesheet and the WebAssembly runtime bootstrap script.

nodes.name: helpers.js
nodes.summary: Client-side JavaScript interop helpers providing a particle/background canvas animation and mouse-tracking pointer effects for interactive UI elements.

nodes.name: animate
nodes.summary: Render loop that clears the canvas, draws and connects animated particles, and schedules the next frame via requestAnimationFrame.

nodes.name: handleMove
nodes.summary: Pointer-move handler that computes cursor position relative to an element and sets CSS custom properties to drive hover/tilt effects.

nodes.name: staticwebapp.config.json
nodes.summary: Azure Static Web Apps configuration defining SPA navigation fallback, MIME type mappings for WebAssembly assets, and global response headers.

nodes.name: AzureReportService.cs
nodes.summary: Core orchestration service that builds the full Azure inventory report by discovering web services, resolving plans, gathering metrics, cost, SSL, config drift, storage/AI/log inventories, orphaned/zombie detection, burn rate, and downtime diagnoses.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large service class composing many timed async ARM/REST calls into one AzureReport aggregate.

nodes.name: AzureReportService
nodes.summary: Aggregates Azure Resource Manager and REST queries into a single AzureReport, coordinating discovery, metrics, cost, inventory, and diagnosis substeps with progress reporting.

nodes.name: RunAsync
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs all report-building steps in sequence with timing and progress reporting, assembles the AzureReport, and computes a delta against the previous report.

nodes.name: DiscoverWebServicesAsync
nodes.summary: Enumerates Azure web sites, static sites, and container apps in the subscription and maps them to raw service descriptors.

nodes.name: ResolveAppServicePlansAsync
nodes.summary: Resolves the App Service Plan and SKU for each web app via concurrent ARM REST calls with throttling and per-request timeouts.

nodes.name: GetMetricsAsync
nodes.summary: Queries Azure Monitor for 7-day request/error/response metrics per app service concurrently and aggregates them into a metrics map.

nodes.name: GetCostAsync
nodes.summary: Calls the Azure Cost Management API for month-to-date spend, parses the tabular response, and ranks the top cost drivers.

nodes.name: CheckSslAsync
nodes.summary: Probes service endpoints to inspect TLS certificates and compute days-until-expiry for SSL monitoring.

nodes.name: GetConfigDriftAsync
nodes.summary: Inspects web app configuration against expected baselines and reports configuration drift issues per service.

nodes.name: GetStorageInventoryAsync
nodes.summary: Builds an inventory of storage accounts from the resource list, enriching each with SKU and capacity details via ARM REST.

nodes.name: GetAiServicesInventoryAsync
nodes.summary: Enumerates Azure AI/Cognitive services and OpenAI deployments, gathering SKU and deployment details into an inventory.

nodes.name: GetLogAnalyticsInventoryAsync
nodes.summary: Collects Log Analytics workspace inventory including retention and ingestion settings from the resource set.

nodes.name: GetOrphanedResourcesAsync
nodes.summary: Detects unattached/orphaned Azure resources (disks, IPs, NICs, etc.) that incur cost without being in use.

nodes.name: GetBurnRateAsync
nodes.summary: Computes daily cost burn rate trends from Cost Management daily breakdown data to project spend.

nodes.name: GetAppInsightsMetricsAsync
nodes.summary: Queries Application Insights for per-service request and failure metrics used in downtime correlation.

nodes.name: DetectZombies
nodes.summary: Identifies zombie web services that have negligible traffic over the metrics window as candidates for decommissioning.

nodes.name: DiffAppsJsonAsync
nodes.summary: Compares discovered live services against the static apps.json catalog and reports added/removed/changed entries.

nodes.name: LoadInfraReviewsForCorrelationAsync
nodes.summary: Loads GitHub infra review/workflow data and correlates the latest workflow runs to discovered services.

nodes.name: ComputeDelta
nodes.summary: Computes the change delta between the current and previous report (status counts, cost) for trend display.

nodes.name: AzureReportStore.cs
nodes.summary: Persistence layer that stores and retrieves Azure reports in Azure Table Storage, with gzip compression, history tracking, and automatic recovery of a missing table.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gzip compression to fit large JSON reports within Table Storage entity limits.

nodes.name: AzureReportStore
nodes.summary: Repository that compresses, saves, loads, and versions AzureReport snapshots in Azure Table Storage with resilient table-recreation handling.

nodes.name: LoadAsync
nodes.summary: Loads the latest stored AzureReport entity from Table Storage and decompresses it, returning a Result wrapping success or failure.

nodes.name: SaveAsync
nodes.summary: Serializes and gzip-compresses an AzureReport and upserts it as the latest entity plus a timestamped history row.

nodes.name: LoadHistoryAsync
nodes.summary: Loads up to a maximum number of historical report snapshots ordered by timestamp for trend analysis.

nodes.name: LoadPreviousAsync
nodes.summary: Loads the second-most-recent report snapshot used to compute deltas against the current run.

nodes.name: GetTableClientAsync
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and caches an Azure Table client from connection string or managed identity, ensuring the table exists.

nodes.name: DowntimeDiagnosisService.cs
nodes.summary: Diagnoses why broken/inactive web services are down by correlating ARM deployments, activity-log stop events, App Insights metrics, and GitHub workflow runs into per-service diagnoses.

nodes.name: DowntimeDiagnosisService
nodes.summary: Service that produces root-cause downtime diagnoses for broken services by combining deployment, activity-log, metrics, and CI signals.

nodes.name: DiagnoseAsync
nodes.summary: Concurrently analyzes each broken service, inspecting deployments and activity logs for stop events and correlating metrics and workflow runs to infer the downtime cause.

nodes.name: IncidentService.cs
nodes.summary: Detects service status transitions between report runs, records incidents to Azure Table Storage, optionally posts a webhook notification, and loads recent incidents.

nodes.name: IncidentService
nodes.summary: Records and retrieves service incident entries, detecting status changes across report history and notifying via webhook.

nodes.name: DetectAndRecordAsync
nodes.summary: Compares the current report's service statuses against history, records new incidents to Table Storage, and posts a webhook when configured.

nodes.name: LoadRecentAsync
nodes.summary: Queries recent incident entries from Table Storage, tolerating a missing table by creating it and returning an empty list.

nodes.name: PostWebhookAsync
nodes.summary: Serializes detected incidents and POSTs them to a configured webhook endpoint, logging on non-success.

nodes.name: PlanRecommendationService.cs
nodes.summary: Analyzes services with their config drift and downtime diagnoses to produce prioritized App Service Plan upgrade/downgrade recommendations with cost estimates.

nodes.name: PlanRecommendationService
nodes.summary: Produces prioritized plan-change recommendations per service based on drift, downtime, custom-domain indicators, and SKU cost tables.

nodes.name: Analyze
nodes.summary: Builds drift and diagnosis lookup maps and produces a priority-ordered list of plan recommendations across all services.

nodes.name: AnalyzeService
nodes.summary: Evaluates a single service's SKU, drift issues, downtime, and custom-domain needs to recommend a target plan with rationale and cost delta.

nodes.name: RawService.cs
nodes.summary: Internal record describing a discovered Azure service before enrichment, with a mapping helper to the public WebService model.

nodes.name: ServicePingerService.cs
nodes.summary: Background hosted service that periodically pings all known service URLs, records up/down telemetry, and caches the latest ping snapshot.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements BackgroundService.ExecuteAsync for a recurring sweep with bounded concurrency.

nodes.name: ServicePingerService
nodes.summary: Hosted background service that sweeps service URLs on an interval, records pinger telemetry, and exposes the current snapshot plus per-service disable toggles.

nodes.name: ExecuteAsync
nodes.summary: Background loop that runs the ping sweep on a configured interval until cancellation.

nodes.name: PingAllServicesAsync
nodes.summary: Loads services from the report store and pings each (excluding disabled ones) concurrently, recording telemetry and caching a snapshot.

nodes.name: PingOneAsync
nodes.summary: Sends a single timed HTTP request to a service URL with a timeout and returns an up/down ping result.

nodes.name: AppKeyVaultSecretManager.cs
nodes.summary: Custom Key Vault secret manager that filters to an app-specific prefix and strips it when mapping secret names to configuration keys.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.Infrastructure.csproj
nodes.summary: Project file for the Infrastructure library declaring Azure SDK, Table Storage, and related package references.

nodes.name: Telemetry.cs
nodes.summary: Static OpenTelemetry meter and instrument definitions (counters/histograms) for Azure OpenAI, GitHub, pinger, and refresh metrics.

nodes.name: Telemetry
nodes.summary: Central static holder of the application Meter and its counters/histograms plus a helper to bucket HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: AppServicePlanInventory.cs
nodes.summary: Shared helper that builds an inventory of PoShared App Service Plans, filtering server farms and counting hosted apps per plan.

nodes.name: BuildPoSharedPlanInventory
nodes.summary: Filters resources to PoShared server farms and projects each into an inventory entry annotated with the count of apps using that plan.

nodes.name: AzureModels.cs
nodes.summary: Shared record/DTO definitions for the entire Azure report domain (web services, metrics, cost, SSL, drift, inventories, diagnoses, recommendations) used across client and server.
nodes.languageNotes: Large set of C# record types serving as the contract between report generation, persistence, and the dashboard UI.

nodes.name: Result.cs
nodes.summary: Generic Result<T> type encapsulating success/failure with value, error message, and optional exception for explicit error handling.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.Shared.csproj
nodes.summary: Minimal project file for the Shared library containing cross-cutting models and result types.

nodes.name: PoUserSession.cs
nodes.summary: Minimal shared record modeling the persisted user session state.

nodes.name: _Imports.razor
nodes.summary: Global Razor using-directive file importing common namespaces for the server host's Razor components.

nodes.name: config
nodes.summary: Azure Developer CLI (azd) environment configuration file recording the active default Azure environment.

nodes.name: api.http
nodes.summary: REST Client request collection for manually exercising the application's diagnostic, health, GitHub, and Azure API endpoints during development.

nodes.name: App.razor
nodes.summary: Root Blazor host document defining the HTML shell, head outlet, stylesheet links, and the Routes component with WebAssembly render mode.

nodes.name: PingerEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Maps minimal-API endpoints that expose the background Pinger service snapshot and a toggle to enable/disable connectivity pinging.

nodes.name: MapPingerEndpoints
nodes.summary: Registers GET/POST routes for the Pinger snapshot and the disabled-state toggle on the web application.

nodes.name: DiagEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the diagnostics API surface: serving the Azure efficiency report, triggering and cancelling background refresh runs over SignalR, and rendering the /diag HTML status page.

nodes.name: MapDiagEndpoints
nodes.summary: Registers all diagnostics routes including report download, refresh start/cancel, history, automation-script download, and the diag page; orchestrates the background AzureReportService run with progress broadcast over the RefreshHub.

nodes.name: GetDiag
nodes.summary: Builds the diagnostics payload by inspecting required/optional configuration keys, masking secrets, and returning either JSON or an HTML status page based on content negotiation.

nodes.name: BuildDiagHtml
nodes.summary: Renders the diagnostics status page as an HTML string from environment info, masked configuration, and report availability.

nodes.name: WantsJson
nodes.summary: Determines whether the caller prefers a JSON response based on the format query parameter and Accept header.

nodes.name: HealthEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Maps health-check and client-config endpoints, reporting application status, dependency checks, and a masked configuration snapshot.

nodes.name: MapHealthEndpoints
nodes.summary: Registers the /health (and alias) and /api/config endpoints returning status JSON, environment, dependency checks, and the model catalog.

nodes.name: MaskValue
nodes.summary: Masks a sensitive configuration value for safe display in diagnostics output.

nodes.name: RefreshSessionManager.cs
nodes.summary: Singleton coordinating a single concurrent Azure report refresh run via a lock and a cancellable token source.

nodes.name: RefreshSessionManager
nodes.summary: Holds the active cancellation token source and lock that serialize background refresh runs, with helpers to set and cancel the active run.

nodes.name: GitHubEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Maps GitHub activity endpoints that fetch repository activity, compute a health score, and cache results per repository.

nodes.name: MapGitHubEndpoints
nodes.summary: Registers the GitHub activity routes on the web application.

nodes.name: InvokeAsync
nodes.summary: Core handler that validates the repo, fetches GitHub activity with rate-limit and caching handling, and returns a computed health score.

nodes.name: GetTrackedAsync
nodes.summary: Returns the set of tracked repositories for the GitHub activity dashboard.

nodes.name: InfraEndpoints.cs
nodes.summary: Maps infrastructure-discovery endpoints that scan GitHub repositories for workflow and IaC files, parse them, and infer deployment topology.

nodes.name: MapInfraEndpoints
nodes.summary: Registers infra-scan routes that aggregate repository workflow and infrastructure metadata for the dashboard.

nodes.name: ScanRepoAsync
nodes.summary: Scans a single repository tree for workflow and infrastructure files and assembles a parsed summary.

nodes.name: FetchAllReposAsync
nodes.summary: Fetches all repositories accessible to the configured GitHub account for infrastructure scanning.

nodes.name: FetchFileContentAsync
nodes.summary: Retrieves and decodes the content of a file from a GitHub repository.

nodes.name: ParseWorkflow
nodes.summary: Parses a GitHub Actions workflow YAML to extract jobs, triggers, and deployment-relevant steps.

nodes.name: ParseInfraFile
nodes.summary: Parses an infrastructure-as-code file (Bicep/Terraform/Docker) to extract resource and deployment hints.

nodes.name: InferDeployment
nodes.summary: Infers the deployment target and topology from parsed workflow and infrastructure metadata.

nodes.name: TryGetGhTokenAsync
nodes.summary: Attempts to resolve a GitHub access token from configuration or the gh CLI for authenticated API calls.

nodes.name: FetchLatestWorkflowRunAsync
nodes.summary: Fetches the most recent GitHub Actions workflow run status for a repository.

nodes.name: GlobalExceptionHandler.cs
nodes.summary: Implements IExceptionHandler to convert unhandled exceptions into structured ProblemDetails responses with logging.

nodes.name: GlobalExceptionHandler
nodes.summary: Global exception handler that logs unhandled exceptions and writes a ProblemDetails JSON response to the client.

nodes.name: RefreshHub.cs
nodes.summary: SignalR hub that broadcasts Azure report refresh progress and completion events to connected dashboard clients.

nodes.name: RefreshHub
nodes.summary: SignalR Hub subclass used as the server-side endpoint for pushing refresh progress to browser clients.

nodes.name: Program.cs
nodes.summary: ASP.NET Core host entry point: bootstraps Serilog, wires Azure Key Vault, OpenTelemetry, SignalR, Radzen, services, middleware, and maps all feature endpoints before running the Blazor Web App.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level statements style Program.cs using minimal hosting; endpoint mapping is delegated to feature MapXxxEndpoints extension methods.

nodes.name: launchSettings.json
nodes.summary: Visual Studio / dotnet launch profiles defining environment variables and application URLs for running the Blazor host locally.

nodes.name: Routes.razor
nodes.summary: Blazor Router component that resolves routes against the host and client assemblies, applying the shared MainLayout and a not-found fallback.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest for the Playwright E2E test project declaring test scripts and the @playwright/test dev dependency.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright configuration that auto-starts the Blazor server, sets the base URL, and defines chromium and mobile-chrome test projects.

nodes.name: diag-api.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E spec verifying the diagnostics API endpoints respond correctly against the running Blazor host.

nodes.name: health.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E spec checking the /health endpoint returns a healthy status from the live server.

nodes.name: static-openapi.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E spec validating that the static OpenAPI document is served correctly.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Playwright E2E test project.

nodes.name: ApiEndpointTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit integration tests exercising health, config, diag-report, az-status, OpenAPI, static-file, and automation-script endpoints against an in-memory test host.

nodes.name: HealthEndpointTests
nodes.summary: Integration tests asserting the /health endpoint status code, content type, and response fields.

nodes.name: ConfigEndpointTests
nodes.summary: Integration tests validating the /api/config endpoint response shape and feature flags.

nodes.name: DiagReportEndpointTests
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering the diagnostics report endpoint status and content type.

nodes.name: AzStatusEndpointTests
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the Azure status endpoint response fields.

nodes.name: ApiSchemaContractTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit integration tests asserting API JSON responses use camelCase property naming across health, config, az-status, and diag-report endpoints.

nodes.name: ApiSchemaContractTests
nodes.summary: Verifies the JSON serialization contract (camelCase property names) of key API endpoints.

nodes.name: AzureReportStoreTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit integration tests for AzureReportStore persistence, covering empty-connection handling, save/load round-trips, history retention, and concurrent saves against Azurite.

nodes.name: AzureReportStoreAzuriteTests
nodes.summary: Tests save/load round-trips and latest-only semantics of AzureReportStore against an Azurite-backed table.

nodes.name: AzureReportStoreHistoryTests
nodes.summary: Tests history retention and concurrent-save behaviour of AzureReportStore.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.Tests.Integration.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the integration test assembly declaring test SDK, xUnit, and project references.

nodes.name: TestWebAppFixture.cs
nodes.summary: WebApplicationFactory-based xUnit fixture that boots the host under the Testing environment and shares it across the integration test collection.

nodes.name: TestWebApp
nodes.summary: Custom WebApplicationFactory configuring the Testing environment for hermetic integration tests.

nodes.name: AppServicePlanInventoryTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit unit test verifying the App Service plan inventory builder filters to PoShared server farms and counts hosted apps correctly.

nodes.name: AppServicePlanInventoryTests
nodes.summary: Unit test class for the App Service plan inventory aggregation logic.

nodes.name: GitHubActivityTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit unit tests for the GitHub activity endpoint covering validation, caching, rate-limit handling, and health-score computation with a stubbed HTTP handler.

nodes.name: GitHubActivityEndpoint_HealthScoreTests
nodes.summary: Unit tests asserting the GitHub repository health-score algorithm across high, low, and edge-case inputs.

nodes.name: GitHubActivityEndpoint_RateLimitTests
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying rate-limit responses and cooldown/cache-poisoning behaviour of the GitHub endpoint.

nodes.name: GitHubActivityEndpoint_ValidationTests
nodes.summary: Unit tests validating repository-input validation of the GitHub activity endpoint.

nodes.name: PoAppSessionTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit unit tests verifying PoAppSession persistence (save, get, clear) against browser local storage.

nodes.name: PoAppSessionTests
nodes.summary: Unit test class for serializing and persisting the application session to browser storage.

nodes.name: PoPunkouterSoftware.Tests.Unit.csproj
nodes.summary: MSBuild project file for the unit test assembly, declaring the test SDK, xUnit and FluentAssertions packages, and project references to the code under test.
nodes.languageNotes: SDK-style .NET test project enabling IsPackable=false and the Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk test runner.

nodes.name: ResultTests.cs
nodes.summary: xUnit test suite verifying the Result<T> value type, covering success construction, failure construction with and without exceptions, and behavioural invariants such as default values and verbatim error preservation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FluentAssertions Should() fluent API and generic Result<T> factory methods across nested test classes grouped by scenario.

nodes.name: Result_SuccessTests
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that Result<T>.Success produces a successful result with the expected value, null error, null exception, and support for null values.

nodes.name: Result_FailureTests
nodes.summary: Test class verifying that Result<T>.Failure marks results unsuccessful, sets the error message, defaults the value, and optionally captures an associated exception.

nodes.name: Result_BehaviourTests
nodes.summary: Test class asserting cross-cutting Result<T> behaviours: success and failure remain distinct, reference-type values keep their instance identity, and error messages are preserved verbatim.

layers.name: Client UI Layer
layers.description: Blazor WebAssembly front end: pages, layout/shared components, the Azure operations dashboard, client session state, CSS, and static client assets.

layers.name: API & Host Layer
layers.description: ASP.NET Core host exposing minimal-API endpoints (Health, Diag, GitHub, Infra, Pinger), the SignalR refresh hub, Blazor server shell, global exception middleware, and the Program.cs entry point.

layers.name: Infrastructure Services Layer
layers.description: Azure integration adapters (report, pinger, incident, downtime, plan recommendation, raw services), Key Vault secret management, and telemetry.

layers.name: Shared Contracts Layer
layers.description: Cross-project models and contracts shared by host, client, and infrastructure, including Result<T>, Azure report models, and user-session types.

layers.name: Testing Layer
layers.description: xUnit unit tests, WebApplicationFactory/Azurite integration tests, and Playwright end-to-end specs with their helpers and test configuration.

layers.name: DevOps & Infrastructure
layers.description: CI/CD pipeline, operational PowerShell scripts, Azure provisioning/identity scripts, and the docker-compose service definition.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation covering architecture, conventions, stack, testing, and operational planning notes.

layers.name: Configuration & Build
layers.description: Solution, MSBuild props, project files, app settings, launch profiles, and static data/config JSON that define build and runtime configuration.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what PoPunkouterSoftware is: a .NET 10 Blazor Web App built from a server host (ASP.NET Core API + Blazor shell), a WebAssembly client, a Shared contracts project, and an Infrastructure project of Azure adapters. It introduces the operational endpoints for Azure, diagnostics, GitHub activity, and infrastructure scanning, with health at /health and diagnostics at /diag, all running locally on HTTP port 8000. Read alongside ARCHITECTURE.md to see how the four projects layer together before diving into code.

tour.title: Server Host Entry Point
tour.description: Program.cs is the ASP.NET Core host entry point and the most depended-upon file in the project. It bootstraps Serilog logging, wires Azure Key Vault and OpenTelemetry, registers SignalR, and maps the application's minimal-API endpoint groups. This file is the runtime spine that every host feature hangs off of, so understanding its startup order frames everything that follows.
tour.languageLesson: ASP.NET Core minimal APIs use a top-level WebApplicationBuilder/WebApplication pair: services are registered on builder.Services, then endpoints are mapped via extension methods like app.MapXxxEndpoints(), keeping each feature's routes in its own file.

tour.title: Health & Diagnostics Endpoints
tour.description: Building on the host wired up in Step 2, these endpoint groups expose the operational surface promised by the README. HealthEndpoints maps /health and the client-config endpoint reporting application and dependency status, while DiagEndpoints serves the Azure efficiency report and triggers and cancels report refreshes under /api/diag/*. Together they are the diagnostic front door used by both the dashboard and the test suite.

tour.title: Operational Endpoint Groups
tour.description: Beyond diagnostics, the host exposes feature-specific endpoint groups that round out the operational API. PingerEndpoints surfaces the background service-ping snapshot and a toggle, GitHubEndpoints fetches repository activity and computes a health score, and InfraEndpoints scans GitHub repositories for workflow and IaC files. Each follows the same minimal-API mapping pattern from Step 3, keeping a clean per-feature folder structure.

tour.title: SignalR Refresh Hub
tour.description: Long-running Azure report refreshes need live progress updates, and that is the job of RefreshHub, a SignalR hub that broadcasts refresh progress and completion events to connected clients. RefreshSessionManager is the singleton that coordinates a single concurrent refresh run using a lock and a cancellation token, ensuring the diag endpoints from Step 3 never start two scans at once. This real-time channel is what keeps the client dashboard in Step 8 live.
tour.languageLesson: A SignalR Hub is a server-side class whose public methods clients can call and which can push messages back to clients over a persistent WebSocket connection, enabling server-initiated progress events rather than client polling.

tour.title: Azure Report Orchestration
tour.description: The Infrastructure project holds the Azure adapters that do the real work behind the endpoints. AzureReportService is the core orchestrator that discovers web services and builds the full inventory report, AzureReportStore persists reports to Azure Table Storage with gzip compression, and ServicePingerService is the background hosted service that periodically pings service URLs to record up/down status. This is where the diagnostics surface from Steps 3-5 gets its data.

tour.title: Diagnosis & Recommendations
tour.description: On top of the raw inventory, the Infrastructure project layers analysis services that turn data into insight. DowntimeDiagnosisService correlates ARM deployments and activity logs to explain why a service is down, IncidentService detects status transitions between report runs and records incidents, and PlanRecommendationService produces prioritized App Service Plan recommendations from config drift and downtime diagnoses. These services feed the operational and portfolio views the dashboard renders later.

tour.title: Shared Contracts & Models
tour.description: The Shared project is the contract layer that both the host and the WASM client compile against, so the same types cross the wire without duplication. AzureModels defines the record/DTO definitions for the entire Azure report domain, while Result<T> is the generic success/failure type used throughout the codebase for explicit error handling. Understanding these records makes the dashboard code in Step 9 immediately readable.
tour.languageLesson: C# record types give value-based equality and concise immutable DTOs, ideal for serialized contracts shared between server and WebAssembly client. A generic Result<T> wraps success/failure plus a value or error, making failure paths explicit instead of relying on exceptions.

tour.title: Client Entry & Landing Page
tour.description: Now switch to the browser side. The Client Program.cs is the Blazor WebAssembly entry point that configures the root components, the typed HttpClient pointing at the host, and client services. Index.razor is the landing/portfolio page that fetches the live Azure app inventory from the endpoints in Step 4 and renders it as a hero portfolio view, your first taste of the data flowing end to end.
tour.languageLesson: Blazor WebAssembly runs C# in the browser via WebAssembly. The client Program.cs uses WebAssemblyHostBuilder to register root components and inject an HttpClient whose BaseAddress targets the host, so Razor pages can call the server's minimal APIs directly.

tour.title: Azure Operations Dashboard
tour.description: The Azure dashboard is the application's centerpiece UI, split across a markup file and several partial-class code files for maintainability. AzureDashboard.razor renders fleet health, charts, and the portfolio table; AzureDashboard.razor.cs is the primary code-behind that loads reports and handles SignalR-driven refresh updates from the hub in Step 5; and the Operations partial computes operational health and cost-watch views from the shared models in Step 8. This is where every earlier layer converges into a live operator view.
tour.languageLesson: C# partial classes let one logical Blazor component span several files (.razor markup plus multiple .cs files), so a large page like the dashboard can separate concerns — charts, operations, portfolio, trends — without one giant file.

tour.title: Automated Testing
tour.description: The project is verified at three levels. Integration tests boot the real host via a WebApplicationFactory fixture (TestWebAppFixture) and ApiEndpointTests exercises the health, config, diag, and OpenAPI endpoints from Steps 3-4 in-process. The Playwright pages.spec.ts E2E spec then drives a real browser to confirm the Home, Azure Dashboard, and Diag pages load, hydrate the Blazor WASM client, and render without JS errors, validating the full stack you have just toured.
tour.languageLesson: WebApplicationFactory<T> from ASP.NET Core spins up the app in-memory for integration tests, giving real HTTP behavior without a network. Playwright complements this with browser-level E2E tests that assert pages render and hydrate correctly.

tour.title: Build & Deployment Pipeline
tour.description: Finally, the capstone: how this all ships. The GitHub Actions deploy.yml workflow builds and deploys the .NET 10 Blazor app to Azure App Service, while docker-compose.yml describes the containerized local/service composition. Together they close the loop from the source you have toured to a running deployment, mirroring the production target the Azure dashboard monitors.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows are YAML files triggered by 'on' events, with 'jobs' running 'steps' in sequence; here the pipeline restores, builds, tests, and deploys to Azure. Docker Compose declares multi-service stacks so the app and its dependencies start together with one command.
